title: PYG medium preparation (2 L)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"71f6s","text":"Recipe for PYG rich growth medium for Acanthamoeba castellanii trophozoites. Adapted from ATCC Medium: 712 PYG w/ Additives https://www.atcc.org/~/media/F7CFD291FAEC4B26AF234A3A9256C1A3.ashx","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":38,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":124,"length":66}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0BB518C3CA0E11EBA9BE0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.atcc.org/~/media/F7CFD291FAEC4B26AF234A3A9256C1A3.ashx"}}}}
steps: To a 2 L bottle, add: 2 g, sodium citrate dihydrate 2 g, yeast extract 40 g, bacto peptone -stir bar
Bring volume to 1.8 L with dH 2 O (for our reagents, requires adding 1.68 L dH 2 O) and place on stir plate (unheated).

Add: 16 mL 0.05 M CaCl 2 x 2H 2 O 20 mL 0.4 M MgSO 4 x 7H 2 O 20 mL 0.25 M Na 2 HPO 4 (anhydrous) 20 mL 0.25 M KH 2 PO 4 (anhydrous) 20 mL 0.005 M Fe(NH 4 ) 2 (SO 4 ) 2 x 6H 2 O All of these nonsterile stock solutions can be made in advance in bulk and stored at room temperature. 0.05 M CaCl 2 x 2H 2 O = 1.838 g in 250 mL dH 2 O 0.4 M MgSO 4 x 7H 2 O = 24.648 g in 250 mL dH 2 O 0.25 M Na 2 HPO 4 (anhydrous) = 8.873 g in 250 mL dH 2 O 0.25 M KH 2 PO 4 (anhydrous) = 8.506 g in 250 mL dH 2 O 0.005 M Fe(NH 4 ) 2 (SO 4 ) 2 x 6H 2 O = 0.196 g in 100 mL dH 2 O
Stir until solution is clear. Check that pH is 6.5 with a pH strip.

Aseptically add 100 mL, 2 M glucose to the above medium. 2 M glucose is made in advance, filter sterilized, and stored at 4ºC. This is made by slowly adding 180.16 g, glucose to a beaker with approximately 200 mL, dH2O while heating and stirring. Once all glucose is dissolved, bring final volume to 500 mL with dH 2 O and filter sterilize (0.22 µm).
Filter sterilize into sterile 250-mL glass bottles using 0.22 µm bottle-top filters.
Store at Room temperature for up to several months.
creator: Carrie A. Flynn
